subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 1.1.1CS.1 Demonstrate understanding and interest in Literature-in-English and make meaningful connections to text and life.
indicator_description: Explain the concepts, types and importance of literature.
examplars:
1. Share different materials, e.g., leaflets, manuals, textbooks on different subjects. Learners brainstorm to identify these as examples of Literature.
2. Through brainstorming, learners in groups discuss the different meanings of literature to arrive at the meaning 'literature is Life.'
3. Show a short skit, drama or film.
4. Guide learners to identify the issues in the skit, drama, film, songs, etc. e.g., who are the characters? What are the places mentioned in the skit? What roles do the characters play in the skit? Is the skit showing a real-life situation?
5. Let learners relate their answers to real life situations to arrive at the meaning of literature as life.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 1.1.1CS.1 Demonstrate understanding and interest in Literature-in-English and make meaningful connections to text and life.
indicator_description: Differentiate between oral and written literature.
examplars:
1. Discuss written Literature and its types, using language for academic purposes.
2. With the use of collaborative learner groups, review and explore knowledge on the different types of Oral literature from different examples of texts.
3. Learners in groups, make a collection of an exhaustive list of types of written and oral literature under each major type.
4. In groups, learners identify the similarities and differences between oral and written literature.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 1.1.1CS.1 Demonstrate understanding and interest in Literature-in-English and make meaningful connections to text and life.
indicator_description: Compare and contrast the relationship between genres in oral and written literature.
examplars:
1. Present different genres of familiar written literature.
2. Have learners interact with the texts, e.g., learners in group, identify literary devices and the elements of genres of literature.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 1.1.1CS.2 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding in the tools of literary analyses.
indicator_description: Use the tools in analysing varied level appropriate texts
examplars:
1. Learners in small groups (mixed ability/gender), make a collection of examples from the different genres.
2. Through talk for learning, learners organise knowledge gained about oral and written literature by highlighting the differences and finding ways of making adaptations between the two genres.
3. Relate the stories to written pieces using KWL charts, mind maps, presentations, etc.
4. Discuss the examples of written texts developed from Oral literature e.g., Ama Ata Aidoo's ANOWA, Efua Sutherland's MARRIAGE OF ANANSEWA.
5. Learners identify genres of Oral literature and turn them into written versions to present in class.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 1.2.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of a variety of prose fictional texts.
indicator_description: Discuss short stories and novels of varied contexts for pleasure
examplars:
1. Review learners' knowledge on features of Oral Prose.
2. In task-based groups, learners are tasked to identify and present examples of Oral Prose (legend, myth etc) and their features.
3. Learners in pairs, brainstorm to discuss types of fiction. Share answers with other pairs for feedback.
4. Through talking point, discuss the features of fiction using the varied examples identified.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 1.2.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of a variety of prose fictional texts.
indicator_description: Make a distinction between a variety of prose fiction texts.
examplars:
1. In groups (mixed ability/gender), identify stories with varied themes online and share with other groups.
2. In mixed ability groups, apply the knowledge on features in categorising the examples selected.
3. Share examples with other groups to critique.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 1.2.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of a variety of prose fictional texts.
indicator_description: Establish the purposes and appeal of a variety of prose texts.
examplars:
1. Use shared reading activities in groups (mixed ability/gender) to identify some common narrative devices e.g., tone, point of view in carousel activities.
2. Using pyramid grouping, learners come out with purposes of reading. e.g., for pleasure, empathy, moral value, etc.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.2.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of a variety of prose non-fiction texts.
indicator_description: Examine the structure of fiction and nonfiction texts
examplars:
1. Review knowledge on features of fiction and non-fiction texts.
2. Learners in groups, take turns to present knowledge on the features of fiction and non-fiction.
3. Through reflection, groups of mixed-abilities select one prose fiction and another prose non-fiction text and examine the reality or otherwise of the subject matter and share their experiences in class.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.2.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of a variety of prose non-fiction texts.
indicator_description: Compare the elements of fiction and non-fiction
examplars:
1. Learners apply effective communication skills to interview male and female biographers or read some biographies/autobiographies and discuss whether there are elements of imagination or fantasy in those works to deepen their love for local and global works of art.
2. Learners present their findings to show what the two types have in common.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.1.1.CS.2 Demonstrate knowledge of how authors apply style and themes in their creative works.
indicator_description: Discuss the development of themes throughout the selected prose texts
examplars:
1. Through annotated texts, teachers initiate discussions using building on what others say to make learners construct meaning from the setting and atmosphere created by the author(e.g., use of description of places, weather, sounds, smells and relevant details) by building on each other's sensorial capabilities.
2. Learners should be conscious of the sensitivities of others and use ethically acceptable language.
3. Learners in pairs present their work to whole class.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.1.1.CS.2 Demonstrate knowledge of how authors apply style and themes in their creative works.
indicator_description: Discuss the development of style in the selected prose texts.
examplars:
1. Teacher leads learners to extend each other's knowledge through follow-up discussions on the theme and style creating the right ambiance and atmosphere of inclusivity.
2. Learners select prose texts and explore the development of style for feedback.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.1.1.CS.2 Demonstrate knowledge of how authors apply style and themes in their creative works.
indicator_description: Discuss how authors use style to bring out themes in their works.
examplars:
1. Learners are tasked to annotate excerpts of texts which depict the setting and atmosphere drawing on the issues of minority and disability; this will improve societal and religious tolerance.
2. In groups (mixed-ability/gender) learners discuss how authors use style to develop themes in selected texts.
3. Groups present their work for colleagues to critique for feedback.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.1.1.CS.2 Demonstrate knowledge of how authors apply style and themes in their creative works.
indicator_description: Discuss authors' use of theme and style in creating non-fictional texts.
examplars:
1. Teacher guides learners to go through selected texts and make records of five or more episodes which they consider important; as characters reveal themselves and others through actions and inactions, making room for confidence building and sensitivity in turn-taking.
2. In groups, through the guidance of the teacher, learners identify the various viewpoints of different narrators using first, second and third person pronouns as they develop their personalities.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.2.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of diversity in themes and style as tools for interpreting narrative text.
indicator_description: Examine more themes in novels through the major incidents and related ideas
examplars:
1. Allow all to explore learning and interact with each other and to build on what others say to identify themes and subthemes. E.g., recount plots of short stories or novels and bring out the themes and subthemes in them.
2. Discuss cross-cultural and diverse themes applying differentiated learning pedagogies.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.2.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of diversity in themes and style as tools for interpreting narrative text.
indicator_description: Discuss the different aspects of style authors use in creating their works
examplars:
1. In groups, identify the narrative voice in texts and evaluate them applying differentiated approaches under the guidance of the teacher.
2. Assign model essays, videos or podcasts to read, watch and listen to identify the voice and also other narrative elements like chronology.
3. Learners share their experiences and question each other's experiences.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.2.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of diversity in themes and style as tools for interpreting narrative text.
indicator_description: Examine the overall impact of the author's narrative devices and the effect on meaning
examplars: 1. Learners apply narrative elements to understand subject matter by answering questions like: a) Where does the story take place? b) Who are the characters? c) Who is telling the story? d) What happens to the characters? e) How do they work to solve the problem? Etc.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.2.2.CS.2 Communicate personal and critical responses to narratives through different contextual factors.
indicator_description: Discuss the steps of critiquing prose texts using strong and exhaustive textual evidence from both explicit and inferential material
examplars:
1. Follow a suggested guide to critique selected texts by building on what others say, e.g. Well-developed setting, Simple/complex plot, Good convincing relatable characters e.g., not perfect, have flaws etc., Interesting/simple/complex plots, Mood/tone/atmosphere, Theme raises questions about life /society/nature etc., Good use of language/underuse/overuse of language devices etc.
2. Learners take turns to build on what others say to promote unity and teamwork. Also, they respect different opinions from others.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.2.2.CS.2 Communicate personal and critical responses to narratives through different contextual factors.
indicator_description: Develop complete essays based on the critique done on the prose texts.
examplars:
1. This will apply concept/mind mapping, participatory feedback etc to develop and write critique essays. Writing guides may be provided through the processes of critical reading, analysing the text and developing a draft. e.g.
2. With the aid of a template, collate the following information and develop: a) Read and Annotate-author's world, some literary devices etc, some thematic statements, some emotions, feelings, ideas etc that the writer projects... b) Write summaries/paraphrases or synopsis etc. c) Identification of related ideas d) Analysing-your personal responses to the ideas e) Evidence of the personal responses

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.2.3.CS.1 Exhibit creative writing skills by producing novels as fictional and non-fictional prose.
indicator_description: Produce summaries of some selected novels based on varied themes.
examplars:
1. Learners should be guided to brainstorm on topical issues such as feminism, abuse, love, etc. in groups/pairs and develop them into themes.
2. As learners brainstorm, it promotes tolerance and respect for others' opinions.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.2.3.CS.1 Exhibit creative writing skills by producing novels as fictional and non-fictional prose.
indicator_description: Produce narrative pieces applying different styles
examplars:
1. Groups organise their ideas and produce a synopsis to guide the writing.
2. Progressively go through the writing process; drafting, revising, editing, evaluation and publishing.
3. Learners must be conscious of others' abilities and work as a team carrying all members along.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.2.3.CS.1 Exhibit creative writing skills by producing novels as fictional and non-fictional prose.
indicator_description: Apply the narrative writing skills to edit and review short stories created
examplars:
1. Select some African and Non-African narratives and make adaptations.
2. Learners change or swap cultural contexts by changing settings. The endings from happy to sad or sad to happy etc.
3. Share work produced among groups and review.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.3.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of a variety of dramatic forms in analysing varied texts.
indicator_description: Identify the forms in varied drama texts
examplars:
1. Review knowledge on elements of drama using Talking Point. Learners in groups present information while those from other groups give feedback and add.
2. In pyramid task groups/mixed ability groups, look for the different forms of drama online. e.g., tragedy, comedy, comedy and tragicomedy. Encourage learners to find these examples for themselves to develop confidence in using ICT tools for learning.
3. Present findings for a whole class discussion. This will lead to learners using knowledge to develop creativity as a team.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.3.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of a variety of dramatic forms in analysing varied texts.
indicator_description: Distinguish between the forms of drama
examplars:
1. Watch popular short films/dramas.
2. Use questions to guide learners in task groups to focus on important issues.
3. Use the diamond nine strategy to help learners evaluate their ideas.
4. Learners avoid personal biases and stereotyping as they discuss issues in the films and dramas watched. Use ethically acceptable language to avoid hurting the sensibilities of others.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.3.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of a variety of dramatic forms in analysing varied texts.
indicator_description: Examine how the forms are developed in varied texts
examplars:
1. Use the 5 Whys strategy to discuss how the forms are developed in varied texts e.g., Text A is a tragedy. Why?
2. Turn to a partner and share your ideas.
3. Organise the ideas together and present them to the class.
4. Watch popular short films / drama.
5. Use questions to guide learners in task groups/mixed ability groups to focus on important issues.
6. Using the diamond nine strategy to help learners evaluate their ideas. Learners will think critically to evaluate the ideas raised.
7. Learners embrace diversity and practise inclusion as they work together.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.3.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of tools of appreciation in analysing varied dramatic texts.
indicator_description: Identify and explain themes, style and language in drama texts
examplars:
1. Think-Pair-Share the themes and elements of style used in drama texts.
2. Think-Pair-Share will enable learners to question and reflect on issues to draw intelligent conclusions.
3. In mixed ability/gender, groups explore and present the different themes and style used in the selected text.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.3.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of tools of appreciation in analysing varied dramatic texts.
indicator_description: Explain how themes, style and language are developed to bring out the main concerns of drama texts
examplars:
1. Learners individually critique their own work using the guide.
2. In pairs, let them discuss the findings of each other's work.
3. Learners incorporate the feedback into essays. Learners will think critically to critique and give feedback to help them learn.
4. As they work together learners show respect to individuals of different beliefs and cultures.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.3.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of tools of appreciation in analysing varied dramatic texts.
indicator_description: Analyse the use of dramatic devices in varied level appropriate drama texts.
examplars:
1. In groups, learners discuss the importance of dramatic devices.
2. In groups, learners use the 5 Whys strategy to appreciate Acts or Scenes.
3. Learners work together embracing diversity and practise inclusion.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.3.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of tools of appreciation in analysing varied dramatic texts.
indicator_description: Appreciate drama texts that reflect diverse context and concerns
examplars:
1. 5 Whys strategy is an inquiry-based model to help learners engage with the problem.
2. Discuss the use of the devices in the development of characterisation in selected drama text.
3. Identify and analyse the use of aside and soliloquy in the development of themes.
4. Learners individually critique their own work using the guide.
5. In groups/individual learning explore how themes and style are developed in a selected text.
6. Individual works are critiqued based on a template to guide learners and monitor their progress.
7. Learners think critically to apply knowledge and skills as they work showing respect by using academic language.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.1.3.3.CS.1 Exhibit knowledge and understanding of analysing, scripting, and performing drama pieces.
indicator_description: Write a drama piece on varied themes depicting real world events
examplars:
1. Watch a popular film or drama on TV.
2. Learners identify themes.
3. Have learners brainstorm the types of real-life situations that can be used for themes.
4. Discuss the elements of drama and style to include in the text.
5. Make a synopsis of what to write
6. In groups, learners develop one scene of the drama using the Process Writing approach.
7. Learners communicate effectively and confidently orally and in written form. They learn from others as they share ideas respecting individual views.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.1.3.3.CS.1 Exhibit knowledge and understanding of analysing, scripting, and performing drama pieces.
indicator_description: Perform parts/full Acts and scenes.
examplars:
1. Learners plan how the drama will be performed. a) Characters b) Costumes c) Stage direction
2. Learners in groups rehearse and perform parts or full drama.
3. Learners as they rehearse learn to value and work in a democratic and inclusive environment

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.1.CS.1 Engage with a variety of poetic forms for pleasure and its aesthetic effect.
indicator_description: Explore the different forms of lyrical poems (Elegy, Ode, Sonnet, Haiku) using interesting level appropriate examples for pleasure.
examplars:
1. Review knowledge on poetic forms.
2. Learners in groups present information on the first set of poetic forms discussed in year one.
3. Groups present their findings.
4. Discuss the concept and forms/types lyrical poetry(e.g., odes, elegy sonnet ) lyrical poetry.
5. Use the features to identify examples of lyrical poems.
6. Learners discuss embracing diversity and inclusion as they learn from each other.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.1.CS.1 Engage with a variety of poetic forms for pleasure and its aesthetic effect.
indicator_description: Distinguish between the features of lyrical poems in context.
examplars:
1. The distinct features of lyrical poems e.g., figurative language, structure, mood, rhyme and rhythm.
2. Listen to audio versions of poems and search for level appropriate interesting examples in groups.
3. Learners think critically and creatively to explore the features used in lyrical poems as they share ideas and learn from others.
4. Brainstorm to elicit Personal Response
5. Play an age-appropriate contemporary song and have students listen and write down the lyrics on the second occasion/listening with appropriate stress and rhythm.
6. Learners think critically to identify the differences within and between the forms/types. Also, as learners write they develop creativity and leadership to express a sense of belongingness, self-awareness and obedience in a team.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.1.CS.1 Engage with a variety of poetic forms for pleasure and its aesthetic effect.
indicator_description: Compare and contrast the use of language in lyrical poetry to bring out its beauty(aesthetic value)
examplars:
1. Explain the features of lyrical poems in context using creative pedagogy to create pleasure.
2. With examples guide learners to discuss the subject matter and express their agreement or disagreements, explaining why.
3. Review literary devices in context to guide discussion on beauty of language. Learners use academic language confidently and effectively.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.1.CS.2 Demonstrate understanding of how style contributes to the meaning of lyrical poems.
indicator_description: Discuss how these elements of style in poems (form, literary devices, diction, tone, mood, imagery etc.) contribute to meaning
examplars:
1. Learners reflect and explore stylistic devices discussed in narrative poems to extend knowledge e.g., imagery, repetition, form, irony etc.
2. Learners in mixed ability/gender groups are assigned different poems to discuss how authors use style to communicate meaning. Learners share their findings with other groups.
3. Learners will develop critical thinking skills as they reflect and explore the devices. The use of groupings will enable learners work as a team, showing respect to others and avoid personal biases and stereotyping affecting the sensibilities of others.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.1.CS.2 Demonstrate understanding of how style contributes to the meaning of lyrical poems.
indicator_description: Analyse the aesthetic qualities of these elements (form, literary devices, diction, tone, mood, imagery) use in a poem to achieve certain effects
examplars:
1. Learners reflect and explore stylistic devices discussed in narrative poems to extend knowledge e.g., imagery, repetition, form, irony etc to develop competencies in the use of the language.
2. Learners in groups, identify the aesthetic qualities of poetic elements.
3. Encourage learners to identify and discuss some of the devices in context.
4. Evaluate them to prioritise their effects/importance and their contribution to meaning.
5. Learners assign literal or deeper meaning from varied perspectives to enhance structure and mood for effect.
6. Use role-play and classroom games to bring out the meaning and style of different lyrical poets/poetic structures e.g., difference between sonnet/ode.
7. Discuss length, rhyme pattern/stanzas, musical/narrative
8. Collaborative learning will enable learners learn from each other and to appreciate the individual academic differences and work to help each other.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.1.CS.2 Demonstrate understanding of how style contributes to the meaning of lyrical poems.
indicator_description: Compare and contrast how different poets experiment with the different poetic devices
examplars:
1. Learners in groups identify and discuss literary devices in context and evaluate how these contribute to meaning, enhance structure and intensify mood.
2. Learners select poems from different poets and compare and contrast their use of poetic devices.
3. Groups share their work with the class.
4. Individual learners select and practise this activity for personal development.
5. Learners will use academic language to write their information. As the learners work in groups, they show respect and empathy

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.2.CS.1 Demonstrate understanding of multiple meanings, central ideas/themes critically in a poem from different perspectives.
indicator_description: Discuss the dominant themes in the selected poems
examplars:
1. Learners revise denotative and connotative meanings to interpret poetry using mind maps and reflective journals e.g., title in the middle.
2. Learners work in groups to discuss dominant themes in selected poems. Groups present their answers for feedback.
3. Individuals work on selected poems.
4. This activity will encourage learners to develop themselves personally as well as work as a team.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.2.CS.1 Demonstrate understanding of multiple meanings, central ideas/themes critically in a poem from different perspectives.
indicator_description: Analyse in detail how themes develop throughout the poems.
examplars:
1. Learners in groups identify key vocabulary/vocabularies that surround the theme(s) by considering the recurring or repetition of words and explaining them denotatively or connotatively etc..
2. Learners discuss the themes based on the vocabulary identified and present their findings in a creative way.
3. Learners develop creativity and work in groups showing respect to each other.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.2.CS.1 Demonstrate understanding of multiple meanings, central ideas/themes critically in a poem from different perspectives.
indicator_description: Examine the overall impact of the poet's specific word choices on meaning and themes in relation to other poetic elements such as imagery, tone, mood, and words with multiple meaning
examplars:
1. Learners are introduced to the concept of diction as style; using a fishbone approach learners identify diction and discuss the effects in enhancing the meaning/themes. Example; 1. creates tone/mood/atmosphere 2. Supports setting/context 3. Supports imagery
2. Learners discuss themes using poetic elements to explain and identify multiple meanings. Work in groups and help learn from peers.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.2.CS.2 Communicate personal and critical responses to poems through different perspectives
indicator_description: Review literary devices and poetic elements in selected poems.
examplars:
1. Teachers develop and manage talking classrooms through think-pair-share.
2. Make use of knowledge of poetic devices to appreciate selected African and non-African poems.
3. Note the following in think-pair-share.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.2.CS.2 Communicate personal and critical responses to poems through different perspectives
indicator_description: Use the knowledge on poetic elements and devices to critique a poem and write out complete essay by citing strong and exhaustive textual evidence
examplars:
1. Follow the PEE format in justifying personal response
2. Follow a suggested guide to critique selected poems a) Where does it take place? b) Who is speaking? c) Is the writing clear/ambiguous etc? d) Are there enough images? e) Are there special uses of sound? f) Is it written in any format?
3. Learners develop critical thinking skills and use language appropriately. They understand the social needs of others.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.3.CS.1 Construct and perform poetic pieces.
indicator_description: Create a stanza of a poem using a simple template as a guide.
examplars:
1. Through one-on-one interactions and drawing on learners' mixed expressive abilities, teacher shares a sample template to guide learners to write a stanza of a poem.
2. The learners use the template to guide them by taking charge of their learning to develop themselves personally.
3. Learners will use language appropriately to avoid hurting others.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 2.4.3.CS.1 Construct and perform poetic pieces.
indicator_description: Edit and perform the poem created in class.
examplars:
1. Learners in groups use the guide to edit their poems 1. Think and decide on what you want to write about. 2. What format do you want to use? 3. What words, rhymes and rhythm do you want to use? 4. Use the Process Approach in writing your poem. 5. Use the Process Approach in writing your poem.
2. Learners in groups plan and rehearse how to perform their poems. Allow introverts to choose the roles to be played to ensure inclusivity.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding in prose elements in interpreting varied prose texts.
indicator_description: Discuss the significance of contexts of texts in shaping the texts' main concerns.
examplars:
1. Review knowledge on structure of fiction, nonfiction and style. Learners in groups, discuss different prose texts and styles. Learners present their findings.
2. In groups discuss a variety of contexts (social, cultural. political etc.) in identifying themes, characters and style as building blocks of prose which can be broken apart and examined individually to give a deeper meaning into the text drawing on learners' social, ethnic, economic etc. backgrounds.
3. In groups, review elements such as setting, symbolism, plot, etc using a variety of creative and innovative ways to cater for individual needs.
4. Explain the processes of comparing or contrasting different texts; consider the choice of words, read and reread full texts or portions whilst taking notes-listing themes side by side, determining main/central themes, and looking for other works with similar themes.
5. Learners will learn to work in teams to develop empathy to make others feel belonged.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding in prose elements in interpreting varied prose texts.
indicator_description: Analyse the elements of prose useful in comparing/contrasting characters
examplars:
1. In groups, learners use Think-Pair-Share to explain the processes of comparing and contrasting different characters; considering the following process: 1. Select two characters you would like to analyse, 2.Reread text to focus on them, 3.Take notes on a list of traits for each character 4.Compare and contrast them side by side.
2. In groups, learners analyse major and minor characters using this process. Share work with another pair.
3. In working in pairs, learners learn to develop empathy, and think critically to develop themselves personally.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the elements of prose and literary devices in analysing varied prose texts.
indicator_description: Examine and articulate main themes in prescribed texts.
examplars:
1. Reflect on literary appreciation as based on reasoning and supported by textual evidence but not a mere summary of literary works.
2. Discuss the relationship between plot, theme, character working together as crucial for developing every story.
3. Learners do this activity in groups.
4. Share with another group for feedback.
5. Learners develop critical skills and work in teams showing respect to others.
core_competencies: CC, CP, PL, CI, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the elements of prose and literary devices in analysing varied prose texts.
indicator_description: Analyse aspects of plot and their effect on readers.
examplars:
1. Learners create storyboards/comic strip style to show all the important events of the plot in some prescribed texts.
2. Learners discuss parts of the plots of selected novels or short stories and match them to different parts of the plot structure.(Exposition, conflict, complication climax and resolution)
3. In groups, learners create physical or digital diagrams of the different parts of the plot and arrange them afterwards to write the whole story line.
4. Learners work together to creatively generate new ideas and contribute to team discussions showing empathy.
core_competencies: CC, CP, PL, CI, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the elements of prose and literary devices in analysing varied prose texts.
indicator_description: Examine the significance of some of the literary devices in appreciating their prescribed text
examplars: 1. Learners discuss the significance of foil, parallelism, diction, mood, foreshadow, in media res, dramatic irony, vignette, flashback, etc. in chapters, excerpts or full texts by following the following processes
core_competencies: CC, CP, PL, CI, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the elements of prose and literary devices in analysing varied prose texts.
indicator_description: Examine the characterisation techniques of the writer.
examplars: 1. Review foil as opposing characters and brainstorm their significance in the novel, refer to knowledge on comparing/contrasting characters to identity foils, consider their contribution to themes, author's style etc.
core_competencies: CC, CP, PL, CI, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the elements of prose and literary devices in analysing varied prose texts.
indicator_description: Develop essays on the critique done on fictional and non-fictional texts.
examplars:
1. Through scaffolding, teacher guides learners to review the steps used in critiquing prose texts.
2. Simple/complex plot
3. Good convincing relatable characters e.g., not perfect, have flaws etc.
4. Interesting/simple/complex plots
5. In groups (mixed-ability/gender) learners use the above to develop essays on fictions and non-fictions.
6. Share and critique essays.
7. Learners develop critical thinking as well helping others to feel belonged.
core_competencies: CC, CP, PL, CI, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the elements of prose and literary devices in analysing varied prose texts.
indicator_description: Apply knowledge of elements of prose and literary devices in critiquing varied prose texts
examplars:
1. Through the use of KWL chart, mind maps, etc., learners brainstorm on ideas from text to discuss the following: o Mood/tone/atmosphere o Theme raises questions about life /society/nature etc. o Good use of language/underuse/overuse of language devices.
2. This will apply concept/mind mapping, participatory feedback etc., to develop and write critique essays. Writing guides may be provided through the processes of critical reading, analysing the text and developing a draft.
3. e.g., with the aid of a template, collate the following information and develop: a) Read and Annotate-author's world, some literary devices etc, some thematic statements, some emotions, feelings, ideas etc., that the writer projects. b) Write summaries/paraphrases or synopsis etc. c) Identify related ideas d) Analysing-your personal responses to the ideas e) Evidence of the personal responses
4. Drafting/Writing a) Evidence of the personal responses b) Writing thesis statement/response to essay prompt c) Using the PEE format to develop paragraphs, assigning one idea to one paragraph, with clear topic sentences d) Develop a concluding paragraph to provide a final judgement, summarise main points and refer back to thesis/essay prompt
5. Learners think critically and apply knowledge creatively. They show empathy and respect.
core_competencies: CC, CP, PL, CI, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.3.CS.1 Demonstrate understanding of the use of writing and dialoguing skills to creatively construct essays and narrative pieces.
indicator_description: Draft and organise arguments with the support of textual evidence for writing or speaking
examplars:
1. Critical thinking and problem solving by reading and rereading excerpts or chapters for students to establish authors' points of view and providing opportunities for learners' personal responses to the authors' points of view.
2. Highlight the importance of textual evidence in arriving at an argument or supporting a stance. Allow learners to express their arguments in open verbal discussions clearly and coherently.
3. Learners think critically to solve problems. Contribute to team discussion using ethically acceptable language.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.3.CS.1 Demonstrate understanding of the use of writing and dialoguing skills to creatively construct essays and narrative pieces.
indicator_description: Use the PEE approach to develop and organise examination style essays
examplars:
1. Analyse samples of marked learners' essays and identify how they respond to essay prompts and their organisation in relation to paragraphing and textual evidence.
2. Learners practice writing sample paragraphs asking students to refer to the good and bad essays analysed through shared writing, scaffolding, four square activity etc.
3. Teacher revises and highlights their knowledge of coherence to unify their essays.
4. In a think pair share activity, learners make use of their annotations, paraphrases, summaries and quotations to support their discussions.
5. Pair work helps learners express belongingness ,self-awareness and obedience in a team.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.2.3.CS.1 Demonstrate understanding of the use of writing and dialoguing skills to creatively construct essays and narrative pieces.
indicator_description: Apply their knowledge of appropriate metalanguage and prose-specific conventions to review or create their own narrative pieces
examplars:
1. Through brainstorming, teacher reviews the use of metalanguage appropriate for writing prose. e.g., run-on-lines, conversational language, personification etc and guide learners to identify such usages.
2. Learners in groups (mixed ability/gender)revise some prose specific elements and review some creative works on their own.
3. Learners create their own narrative pieces (short stories. novels etc.) using prose specific conventions such as chapters, narrator, etc. with the help of writing guides and templates.
4. Learners allocate tasks between members and groups and recognise others that need help.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.3.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of a variety of dramatic forms in analysing varied texts.
indicator_description: Discuss the connection between dramatic forms and themes
examplars:
1. Review knowledge on forms and elements of drama and style. Learners work in groups on given tasks and present their findings for revision. Learners from other groups add information.
2. Watch plays and identify a) Forms of drama b) Themes in the plays.
3. This activity requires learners to think critically to apply knowledge on forms of drama and themes.
4. In Task Groups, discuss and creatively present them in Role Play e.g. 'I am Comedy' and will always give you fun.
5. In communicating with other learners, they learn to use language confidently and effectively
core_competencies: CC, CP, CI, PL, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.3.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of a variety of dramatic forms in analysing varied texts.
indicator_description: Compare and contrast the different dramatic forms in varied texts
examplars:
1. Brainstorm the different forms of drama in a whole class activity.
2. In mixed ability/gender-based groups compare and contrast dramatic forms in two texts. Occasional use of same sex groupings to encourage competitive learning practiced in mixed gender institutions.
3. Groups present their findings in a well written essay.
4. In group carousel activities, critique works to identify the best based on a template developed by the class.
5. This is the beginning of creative performance. Introverts should be encouraged to try, however, allow those who are ready to present. Gender based groups can be used occasionally to encourage inclusivity in a healthy competition throughout the session.
core_competencies: CC, CP, CI, PL, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.3.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of tools of appreciation in analysing varied dramatic texts.
indicator_description: Appreciate drama texts for their empathetic effects using the dramatic tools.
examplars:
1. In Task Groups review dramatic tools. The dramatic tools are creatively presented in skits/songs/poetry by the learners.
2. Select important speeches of main characters to appreciate their empathetic effects. Work in pairs and discuss with another pair. Reflect and use strategy to work on other speeches individually.
3. Work together to develop empathy and respect to help others feel belonged.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.3.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of tools of appreciation in analysing varied dramatic texts.
indicator_description: Use tools of appreciation and dramatic elements in analysing varied texts
examplars:
1. Using diamond nine, discuss the dramatic elements with examples from a text.
2. In mixed-ability groups analyse a text using tools of appreciation and dramatic elements. Present answers as seminar papers to the class. Independently analyse a text using tools of appreciation and dramatic elements.
3. Learners develop empathy, respect and critical thinking skills, take leading roles to work with others and lend support to one another in group work.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.3.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of tools of appreciation in analysing varied dramatic texts.
indicator_description: Critique selected speeches of selected characters and how they influence the development of the plot
examplars:
1. In a whole class activity, discuss the stages of critiquing characters' speeches. Use concept mapping to help organise the steps and ideas.
2. In mixed-ability groups, follow steps to critique a speech. Share and discuss with other groups and the whole class.
3. Individually critique selected speeches and monitor progress using a guide.
4. Learners develop critical thinking skills, articulate and explain one's feelings in a group situation.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.3.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of tools of appreciation in analysing varied dramatic texts.
indicator_description: Appreciate varied drama texts for their empathetic effects
examplars:
1. In task groups, appreciate a given text for its empathetic effects. Present the work to the class.
2. Alone, appreciate a text for its empathetic effects. Share your work with a partner. Assess and monitor your work using the guide.
3. Learn from others and exchange ideas and lend support to others in the group.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.3.3.CS.1 Exhibit knowledge and understanding of analysing, scripting, converting and performing drama pieces,
indicator_description: Use the skills acquired to create drama pieces
examplars:
1. In groups (friendship or gender based) write out a story based on a contemporary issue. Write a synopsis to guide the writing process. Use the process writing approach.
2. Learners work in groups to embrace cultural diversity and inclusion to lend support to others.
core_competencies: CI, CP, CC, PL, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.3.3.CS.1 Exhibit knowledge and understanding of analysing, scripting, converting and performing drama pieces,
indicator_description: Convert drama pieces into stories
examplars:
1. Convert the story into drama. Choose a dramatic form. Elements of drama. Remember the process writing approach may be useful still.
2. Learners in groups use the process approach to convert stories to drama.
3. Learners work in groups to lend support to others.
core_competencies: CI, CP, CC, PL, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.3.3.CS.1 Exhibit knowledge and understanding of analysing, scripting, converting and performing drama pieces,
indicator_description: Perform the drama pieces created
examplars:
1. Perform the drama. Using the Diamond Nine to help prioritise the important things to guide the performance, e.g., characters, costumes.
2. Share the roles, rehearse and perform. Learn from different cultures,take turns to share ideas.
core_competencies: CI, CP, CC, PL, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.4.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of how authors apply the elements of poetry in their works.
indicator_description: Review the elements of poetry
examplars:
1. Learners in groups (mixed-ability plus gender) brainstorm the elements of poetry. Learners share ideas by communicating confidently and effectively using ethically academic language.
2. In groups, they learn to respect others, different beliefs, religion and cultures.
3. Learners think critically and creatively to organise their work for presentation. Learners show empathy and respect while learning to take turns at contributing to discussion.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.4.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of how authors apply the elements of poetry in their works.
indicator_description: Discuss the use of elements of poetry in varied selected poems
examplars:
1. Brainstorm the elements of poetry and different poetic forms.
2. In task groups, explore and present how the elements of poetry are used in the selected poems. Each group works on a poem. Present, paying attention to presentation skills.
3. Learners in groups lend help to others with respect

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.4.1.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of how authors apply the elements of poetry in their works.
indicator_description: Compare and contrast how different poets use the elements to create meaning
examplars:
1. Use the 5 Whys to explore the different poets' use of elements of poetry in creating meaning. Two pairs discuss and critique each other's work to present to the class.
2. Through talking point compare and contrast the differences between two poets in their use of elements of poetry to create meaning. Individually write a full easy and monitor with the assessment guide.
3. Learners use ethically acceptable language and embrace diversity while providing support to others.

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.4.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of multiple meanings, central ideas, themes, language and elements of poetry critically in poems from different perspectives.
indicator_description: Discuss how writers explore the elements in making meaning in their works
examplars:
1. In task groups, share poems from the selected texts. Examine how the author explores the elements of poetry used in the chosen poem. Have another group work on the same poem.
2. Write an essay to be presented in a class seminar. Class critique for groups to include the feedback. Individuals reflect on poems and do a similar activity.
3. Follow a template to monitor progress of work for improvement.
4. Learners in groups embrace diversity and inclusivity using academic language as they support each other.
core_competencies: CC, CP, CI, PL, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.4.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of multiple meanings, central ideas, themes, language and elements of poetry critically in poems from different perspectives.
indicator_description: Compare how different authors create multiple meanings using the poetic elements in same poetic forms
examplars:
1. In mixed-ability groups, have groups choose two authors each from the selected text.
2. Compare how the different authors create multiple meanings in the same poetic form using poetic elements.
3. Groups share their work with each other.
4. Groups present their work in well written essays in a class seminar. Individuals work on a similar activity.
5. Use a guide to help monitor progress and improve upon work.
6. Learners give help and support to others in groups ,use ethically acceptable language in a way to avoid hurting the sensitivities of others.
core_competencies: CC, CP, CI, PL, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.4.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of multiple meanings, central ideas, themes, language and elements of poetry critically in poems from different perspectives.
indicator_description: Compare how different authors create multiple meanings using poetic elements in different poetic forms.
examplars:
1. In task groups, review knowledge on poetic elements using pyramid discussion.
2. Write the new ideas in a learning journal.
3. Work in the same groups this time examining how different authors create multiple meanings using poetic elements in different poetic forms.
4. Organise your points using the 5 whys strategy.
5. Share your findings with another group.
6. Write a full essay and present in a class seminar.
7. The class critiques the easy using the assessment guide.
8. Learners develop critical thinking skills, avoid personal biases and stereotyping to hurt the sensitivities of others to make others feel belonged.
core_competencies: CC, CP, CI, PL, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.4.2.CS.1 Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of multiple meanings, central ideas, themes, language and elements of poetry critically in poems from different perspectives.
indicator_description: Critique the works of different authors using the skills gained.
examplars:
1. Work as individuals to critique the works of different authors.
2. In your Reading/Journal write the varied meanings identified in each author's work within the same poetic form.
3. Write an essay using the PEE strategy.
4. Use the assessment guide to monitor progress.
5. Now compare same poetic forms in different works
6. Learner learns to develop personal skills and prepare for continuous learning.
core_competencies: CC, CP, CI, PL, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.4.3.CS.1 Create and perform poetic pieces.
indicator_description: Create poems of varied lengths on diverse issues
examplars:
1. In mixed-ability groups, go online to select different types of poems of varied lengths e.g., Ballads, Elegy.
2. Study these poems to guide the group to create poems of varied themes.
3. Follow a simple procedure and use the process approach to guide the writing.
4. Share the group's finished product with another group to reflect and critique it.
5. Publish poems.
6. Learners learn to take turns, lead and contribute to discussions, learn from each other and lend support to others.
core_competencies: CC, CP, CI, PL, DL, CG

subject: Literature in English
content_standard: 3.4.3.CS.1 Create and perform poetic pieces.
indicator_description: Perform the poems created through diverse modes
examplars:
1. In groups select a mode to perform the poem created. Write a plan to guide the performance. Share roles and rehearse.
2. Introverts should be encouraged to take part to ensure inclusivity while using ethically acceptable language during rehearsal.
core_competencies: CC, CP, CI, PL, DL, CG
